narrative.description: Marble Madness (1984) is a acclaimed arcade title recognized in retro gaming lists for its distinctive mechanics, pacing, and lasting influence on the genre.
narrative.core_loop: Survive short intense sessions chasing high scores with limited lives.

mechanics.map_notes: Core momentum based speed loop defines Marble Madness's identity.
mechanics.expression: Character velocity accumulates from slopes, boosts, and sustained input, making speed a core traversal resource.

mechanics.map_notes: Core life based retry system loop defines Marble Madness's identity.
mechanics.expression: A failure-buffer system in which the player is granted a limited number of retries, losing one life upon failure and restarting from a previous checkpoint.

mechanics.map_notes: Core linear stage gauntlet loop defines Marble Madness's identity.
mechanics.expression: Progression moves through a fixed sequence of confined stages without hub backtracking.

mechanics.map_notes: Depletable Health Pool supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: A survival mechanic where the player character possesses a finite quantity of health points that decrease when taking damage, with depletion resulting in failure.

mechanics.map_notes: Telegraphed Threat Loop supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: A combat encounter structure in which an enemy signals an incoming attack through a distinct visual or audio cue before the attack resolves.

mechanics.map_notes: Patterned Boss Encounter supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: A boss fight design in which the enemy executes a deterministic or semi-deterministic set of behaviors including telegraphed attacks and movement routines.

mechanics.map_notes: Optional Content Layering supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: Hidden rooms, passages, or content zones exist off main paths, requiring players to investigate suspicious environments.

mechanics.map_notes: Mid Level Checkpoint supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: A save point within a stage resets the player to that position on death rather than restarting the entire level.

mechanics.map_notes: Environmental Hazard Navigation supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: A traversal mechanic in which the level space contains persistent damaging or lethal obstacles that the player must avoid through precise movement.

mechanics.map_notes: Combo Trick Scoring supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: Chained tricks and aerial maneuvers multiply score within a combo timer during skating or stunt gameplay.

mechanics.map_notes: Input Aligned Movement supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: Movement responds directly and immediately to input direction with minimal inertia, optimized for top-down or free-aim combat spaces.

mechanics.map_notes: Instant Fire Attack supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: The character executes an attack immediately upon input, with no charge, delay or conditional cost.

mechanics.map_notes: Multi Phase Boss Battle supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: A combat structure where boss encounters transition through discrete stages with distinct attack patterns, behaviors, or environmental conditions when health thresholds are reached.

mechanics.map_notes: Boss Gated Progression supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: A progression structure in which access to subsequent content areas, abilities, or game states is conditionally locked behind the defeat of a designated boss.

mechanics.map_notes: Sector Clear Progression supports the main loop in Marble Madness.
mechanics.expression: Levels advance when a quota of enemies is destroyed or a sector boss is defeated before moving to the next zone.

design_intent.player_fantasy: A arcade experience centered on Marble Madness.
